cards.back: It is a shorter and more readable way to represent binary numbers.
It happens when a binary value is too large to fit in the available number of bits.

cards.front: What is the difference between validation and verification?
cards.back: Validation checks that data is sensible and in the right format; verification checks that it was entered accurately.

cards.front: Name two data structures from the specification.
cards.back: Records and one- or two-dimensional arrays.
